shownotes: Her and I (Slow Jam 2) was played for the first time known since 2015-09-03. (351 show gap) KGLW.net Staff Notes: The setlist for this show is believed to be complete. Planet B, Her and I (Slow Jam 2) and Self-Immolate were confirmed by audio/video recordings. The remaining songs were confirmed via contemporaneous discussions through various social media, but any additional setlist notations that require audio confirmation (guest appearances, segues, quotes or teases) may be incomplete. If anyone has any further information or, ideally, a complete or conclusive recording please contact KGLW.net staff at setlists@kglw.net.

KGLW.net Staff Notes: The setlist for this show is believed to be complete. All songs were confirmed by audio/video recordings except for This Thing which was confirmed via the written setlist and contemporaneous discussions through various social media, but any additional setlist notations that require audio confirmation (guest appearances, segues, quotes or teases) may be incomplete. Additionally, KGLW.net staff were able to confirm that Parts I and II of The River were played, however we are unable to confirm whether this was a complete performance of the song or not. If anyone has any further information or, ideally, a complete or conclusive recording please contact KGLW.net staff at setlists@kglw.net.

Rattlesnake was introduced by Joey as “the most annoying song ever written”. After Am I In Heaven Joey said “We’re King Gizzard and the Lizard Wizard which is the stupidest name for a band”. Am I In Heaven contained Rattlesnake and Alter Me quotes and teases and Boogieman Sam teases. KGLW.net Staff Notes: The setlist for this show is believed to be complete. Rattlesnake, Cyboogie and Am I In Heaven? were confirmed by an audience recording. The remaining songs were confirmed via contemporaneous discussions through various social media, but any additional setlist notations that require audio confirmation (guest appearances, segues, quotes or teases) may be incomplete. If anyone has any further information or, ideally, a complete or conclusive recording please contact KGLW.net staff at setlists@kglw.net.

If anyone has any further information or, ideally, a complete or conclusive recording please contact KGLW.net staff at setlists@kglw.net.

This was the first 3 hour marathon-like concert, performed for fundraising and awareness of recent Australian bushfires, and the last concert performed with drummer Eric Moore. Evil Star intro song played from house speakers before the show. I'm Not in Your Mind contained a Cellophane tease. Cellophane contained Alter Me teases and quotes. Wah Wah contained The River teases and quotes. Willoughby's Beach was played for the first time known since 2016-06-25. (280 shows) Sea of Trees was played for the first time since 2017-04-02 (214 shows) and contained Head On/Pill teases. Work This Time contained a guitar solo. Superbug was dedicated to the Coronavirus.

“Melding the worlds of music and cinema, Hear My Eyes is an ongoing series which tasks musicians with composing and performing live scores for some of film’s most evocative works.

For its fifth iteration, Hear My Eyes brought together one of its most exciting collaborations to date. Assembling members from King Gizzard & The Lizard Wizard, Fia Fiell, Bullant and Cold Hands Warm Heart along with Leah Senior and the Melbourne Chamber Choir to compose and perform a brand new score for Dario Argento’s technicolour nightmare Suspiria.” - Beat Magazine. Full Review Below
